channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Researchers say closing a top USDA research lab will slow responses to honeybee deaths
item.description: When beekeepers saw honeybee die-offs last year, experts at a USDA research center stepped in to help. The Trump administration plans to close the facility, and beekeepers and scientists are worried.
item.content:encoded: When beekeepers saw honeybee die-offs last year, experts at a USDA research center stepped in to help. The Trump administration plans to close the facility, and beekeepers and scientists are worried.
item.dc:creator: Macy Byars

item.title: Poll: Most Americans say Trump deserves blame for high gas prices
item.description: The new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds that 8 in 10 Americans say gas prices are straining their household budgets.
item.content:encoded: The new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds that 8 in 10 Americans say gas prices are straining their household budgets.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Antisemitism is a campaign issue in UK regional vote
item.description: After a spate of attacks on Jews in Britain, politicians there are accusing each other of antisemitism ahead of local elections.
item.content:encoded: After a spate of attacks on Jews in Britain, politicians there are accusing each other of antisemitism ahead of local elections.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: 'Project Freedom' aimed at wrangling control of Hormuz ends after less than 48 hours
item.description: Trump calls off an effort to take control of Strait of Hormuz from Iran, citing progress on talks, but the blockade remains.
item.content:encoded: Trump calls off an effort to take control of Strait of Hormuz from Iran, citing progress on talks, but the blockade remains.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Ohio candidates expected to focus on cost of living concerns ahead of midterms
item.description: Ohio's primary results are in setting up what's expected to be expensive contests for the state's governor and U.S. Senate and House races that could help determine the balance of power in Congress.
item.content:encoded: Ohio's primary results are in setting up what's expected to be expensive contests for the state's governor and U.S. Senate and House races that could help determine the balance of power in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Karen Kasler

item.title: How the petrodollar regime came to be, and what losing it would mean for the U.S.
item.description: The global oil market has supported the U.S. dollar for over 50 years. But the system shows signs of cracking under the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: The global oil market has supported the U.S. dollar for over 50 years. But the system shows signs of cracking under the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: How the 1994 World Cup kicked off America's love affair with soccer
item.description: Looking back at the 1994 World Cup in the U.S. and its lasting influence on American soccer.
item.content:encoded: Looking back at the 1994 World Cup in the U.S. and its lasting influence on American soccer.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Indiana primary results show Trump's power over the party
item.description: President Trump tried to push out Indiana state senate candidates who rejected his redistricting plan. Tuesday's results are in and he took down most of the incumbents who opposed this plan.
item.content:encoded: President Trump tried to push out Indiana state senate candidates who rejected his redistricting plan. Tuesday's results are in and he took down most of the incumbents who opposed this plan.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Beck

item.title: Meet the Tiny Desk Contest finalists: The King Will Come
item.description: Tiny Desk Contest judge Bobby Carter introduces us to finalists The King Will Come, a 15-member group based in Utah.
item.content:encoded: Tiny Desk Contest judge Bobby Carter introduces us to finalists The King Will Come, a 15-member group based in Utah.

item.title: Iran steps up executions of prisoners under cover of war
item.description: Human rights experts note a "horrific escalation" of executions in Iran in recent months.
item.content:encoded: Human rights experts note a "horrific escalation" of executions in Iran in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren
